mechanism: Inhibits FLT3-ITD and other tyrosine kinases (e.g., BCR-ABL), suppressing downstream signaling
additional_notes: Active against resistant FLT3 mutants; also targets ABL, PDGFR, VEGFR; aka AP24534

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, VEGFR, PDGFR and other RTKs to reduce AML cell signaling
additional_notes: Early FLT3 inhibitor with activity in FLT3-ITD AML; aka SU11248

mechanism: Inhibits MET and VEGFR2 and also targets FLT3 (including some resistance mutants)
additional_notes: Also known as XL184; preclinical FLT3-ITD AML activity reported

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL kinases and related signaling that supports AML proliferation
additional_notes: Used in CML; shows activity and synergy in AML models

mechanism: Reversible 26S proteasome inhibition leading to proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as VELCADE; AML cell line sensitivity reported

mechanism: Irreversible 20S proteasome inhibition causing accumulation of misfolded proteins
additional_notes: Also known as KYPROLIS; AML lines show susceptibility

mechanism: Pan-HDAC inhibition (class I/II), inducing histone acetylation and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; synergy with FLT3 inhibitors reported

mechanism: Class I selective HDAC inhibition leading to apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as FK228; potent class I HDAC inhibitor

mechanism: Rapalog that inhibits mTORC1 signaling to reduce protein synthesis and proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; often cytostatic but can be cytotoxic in AML

mechanism: Rapalog that inhibits mTOR signaling and AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as CCI-779; related to sirolimus/rapamycin class

mechanism: Blocks JAK-STAT signaling pathway that supports AML survival
additional_notes: Used for myelofibrosis/PV; activity in AML subsets and combinations

mechanism: Inhibits SYK kinase (via active metabolite R406), impairing pro-survival signaling
additional_notes: Active metabolite R406 mediates activity; preclinical AML efficacy including FLT3-ITD contexts

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial ribosomal translation, selectively impairing AML cell metabolism
additional_notes: Glycylcycline antibiotic; selective toxicity to AML cells/stem cells reported

mechanism: D2 receptor antagonist that inhibits STAT5 signaling downstream of FLT3-ITD
additional_notes: Repurposed for STAT5 inhibition in AML (MOLM-13/MV4-11)

mechanism: Chelates iron and inhibits iron-dependent enzymes, inducing apoptosis in AML
additional_notes: Also referred to as CPX; disrupts iron-dependent pathways

mechanism: In presence of copper, forms Cu–DDC complex that targets NPL4/proteasome
additional_notes: Activity requires physiological copper (e.g., CuCl2 supplementation); aka DSF

mechanism: Disrupts Wnt/β-catenin and mTOR signaling and perturbs cellular energetics
additional_notes: Reported anti-leukemia activity; consider solubility/formulation for assays
